title: Pelvic Splint
content_markdown:
#### Indications
- Patients suspected of having a pelvic fracture, particularly if hypotensive.
- Patients with significant decreased LOC where pelvic fracture cannot be excluded.

#### Procedure

- Position the patient supine and remove any clothing that will be under the binder. Underwear can remain in place but avoid bunching of material under binder.

- Unfold sling with white surface facing up.

- Place white side of sling beneath patient at level of buttocks (greater trochanter/symphysis pubis) (see **Figure 1**).

Figure 1

- Firmly close sling by placing black Velcro side of flap down on the black Velcro strip. Fold back material as needed. Try to place buckle close to midlines (see **Figure 2**).

Figure 2

- Grab orange free handle on outer surface of flap and release from flap by pulling upward.

- With or without assistance firmly pull both orange handles in opposite directions to tighten sling (see **Figure 3**).

Figure 3

- Keep pulling free handle until you feel or hear the buckle lock or click.

- As soon as the buckle clicks, maintain tension and firmly press orange handle onto the black Velcro strip. A second click may be heard after the sling is secured (see **Figure 4**).

Figure 4

- To remove sling, lift orange free handle away from flap by pulling upward. Maintain tension and slowly allow sling to loosen. Do not cut sling off patient (see **Figure 5**).

Figure 5

#### Contraindications
- Not to be used on children/adolescents.

#### Specific Notes
- ACTAS carries the medium size belt that fits 95% of the adult population.
- Only to be applied on supine patients.
- Replace splint on a one for one swap with the hospital (i.e. not single use unless significantly contaminated).
- May be applied over clothing.
